findings.summary: git show --name-only prints nothing for a merge commit, so [].every(isReviewRecord) was vacuously true: candidateShas walked through merges down the first-parent lineage and accepted another PR's review record, passing a merge of unreviewed code. The same finding's other half: actions/checkout defaults to GitHub's synthetic merge ref, whose second parent carries the reviewed commit, so the gate would have red-lighted every honest PR.

acceptedBy was checked for truthiness only, so an author could waive their own blocking finding with acceptedBy: 'me' while ADR-0052 claimed only the operator could. Waivers now require a name in reviews/OPERATORS, itself an implementation path.
scenarios/ matched wholesale, so a PR committing nothing but L4 evidence (the project's own DoD workflow) was forced to carry an adversarial code review. A gate that blocks honest work is a defect.
Two validateRecord rules (no findings array; finding without id/summary) had no selftest case.
Implementation path regexes were case-sensitive; a variant-cased path dodged the gate.
docs/V2-ROADMAP.md still instructed the reader to run the deleted /v2-review command.

REDESIGN could not be recorded at all, and the bidirectional mismatch check would have rejected an honest FIX-FIRST over major findings alone. The invariant is now only that MERGE may not be asserted over an unresolved blocking finding.
A non-string acceptedBy threw TypeError out of isOperator: fail-closed, but a stack trace instead of a finding.

Six of seven EXEMPT_PATHS entries were inert (disjoint from IMPL_PATHS); deleting them reddened nothing, and the baseline case named 'reviews/ is exempt' passed for an unrelated reason. A list that read as protection and did nothing, with a test that read as coverage and proved nothing -- inside the gate built to catch that. EXEMPT_PATHS now holds only the entry that shadows an IMPL match; the rest are asserted as behaviour.
isImplementation did not normalise a leading ./ and did not fail closed on a path containing a .. segment.

candidateShas guards a parentless-root rev-parse throw, but the guard has no fixture: it is unreachable through the wired adapter, since base is always a merge-base and therefore an ancestor of HEAD. Disclosed rather than covered by a fixture written only to move a number. The reviewer's exhaustive mutation sweep of all 26 rules found this to be the ONLY rule whose revert reddens no case.
